article.description: — Damian Lillard hit a driving layup with 3.9 seconds left to give the Milwaukee Bucks a 101-100 victory over Houston that snapped the Rockets’ five-game winning streak Monday night.
article.headline: Bucks win 101-100 on Lillard's late basket to snap Rockets' 5-game win streak

images.caption: Bucks coach Doc Rivers heaps praise on Damian Lillard for his clutch play in a win against the Rockets.
images.name: Rivers on Lillard's winner: 'Dame's just got great courage'

article.linkText: Bucks win 101-100 on Lillard's late basket to snap Rockets' 5-game win streak
article.story: MILWAUKEE -- — Damian Lillard hit a driving layup with 3.9 seconds left to give the Milwaukee Bucks a 101-100 victory over Houston that snapped the Rockets’ five-game winning streak Monday night. Lillard scored 18 points after missing the previous three games while in the concussion protocol. The Rockets rallied from a 14-point, third-quarter deficit to take the lead early in the fourth quarter and stayed ahead until Lillard’s decisive basket. Giannis Antetokounmpo blocked a shot by Houston's Alperen Sengun with 20 seconds left. Houston called timeout after Lillard's layup. After the Bucks fouled with 2 seconds left, Houston’s Fred VanVleet tried inbounding the ball to Sengun, who couldn’t catch it cleanly under the basket. VanVleet unsuccessfully tried passing to Jabari Smith Jr. as the horn sounded. Brook Lopez had 27 points, 10 rebounds and four blocks for Milwaukee. Antetokounmpo had 20 points and 14 rebounds. VanVleet scored 26 points, Jalen Green 21 and Sengun 18 for Houston. Takeaways Rockets: One night after a 143-107 victory at Chicago in which they were one point shy of the NBA’s highest scoring total this season, the Rockets were held to their fewest points of the season. Bucks: Since a 2-8 start, Milwaukee has won three of four. Lopez briefly went in the locker room after taking a hard fall early in the second quarter, but he returned later that period and delivered one of his best games of the season. Key moment The Bucks trailed 100-96 when A.J. Green buried a 3-pointer to cut the margin to one with 1:17 left. Key stat Houston shot just 9 of 32 on 3-pointers and had its lowest field-goal percentage (.400) of the season. Up next Both teams play Wednesday, with the Rockets hosting Indiana and the Bucks hosting Chicago. ------ AP NBA: https://apnews.com/hub/nba

articles.description: The San Antonio Spurs enter Game 1 of the NBA Finals against the New York Knicks as the favorites to win the NBA championship.
articles.headline: NBA championship and Finals MVP odds: Spurs favored vs. Knicks entering Finals

images.caption: Victor Wembanyama (center left) and the San Antonio Spurs are favored entering the NBA Finals.
images.credit: Jesse D. Garrabrant/NBAE via Getty Images
images.name: Victor Wembanyama [1296x729]

images.caption: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander and Victor Wembanyama are Nos. 1 and 2 in the Finals MVP odds, respectively.
images.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander [1296x729]

images.alt: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander
images.caption: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander and the Thunder are back to being odds-on title favorites after taking a 3-2 series lead over the Spurs.
images.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander [608x342]

images.alt: Jalen Brunson
images.caption: Jalen Brunson has the New York Knicks in the NBA Finals for the first time since 1999.
images.name: Jalen Brunson [608x342]

images.alt: New York Knicks guard Landry Shamet (L) defends Cleveland Cavaliers guard Donovan Mitchell (R) during game 3 of the NBA East Conference Finals between the Cleveland Cavaliers and the New York Knicks in Cleveland, Ohio, USA, 23 May 2026.
images.caption: The Knicks are looking to end the series against the Cavaliers tonight, as they have the second-shortest title odds and are an overwhelming favorite to win the East.
images.name: Knicks/Cavs [1296x729]

images.alt: Jalen Brunson
images.caption: The Knicks are now heavy favorites to win the Eastern Conference after taking a 3-0 series lead over the Cavs.
images.credit: Nathaniel S. Butler/NBAE via Getty Images
images.name: Jalen Brunson [608x342]

images.alt: Oklahoma City Thunder guard Shai Gilgeous-Alexander (2) takes a shot as San Antonio Spurs guard Stephon Castle (5) defends during the first quarter during game two of the western conference finals for the 2026 NBA playoffs at Paycom Center.
images.caption: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander how has even odds to win the Finals MVP.
images.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander  [1296x729]

images.alt: VIctor Wembanyama
images.caption: VIctor Wembanyama poured in 41 points and grabbed 24 boards in the Spurs' thrilling Game 1 win.
images.name: Victor Wembanyama, Jalen Williams [608x342] - Copy

images.alt: Oklahoma City Thunder guard Shai Gilgeous-Alexander (2) looks for an outlet against San Antonio Spurs guard Stephon Castle (5) during the first half of an NBA basketball game, Tuesday, Jan. 13, 2026, in Oklahoma City.
images.caption: The Thunder enter the Western Conference Finals as the odds-on favorite to win the NBA championship.
images.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander [1296x729]

images.alt: Detroit Pistons guard Cade Cunningham (2) shoots over Cleveland Cavaliers guard Sam Merrill (5) in the second half during game five of the second round of the 2026 NBA Playoffs at Little Caesars Arena.
images.caption: Cade Cunningham and the Pistons are favored heading into Game 7.
images.name: Cade Cunningham [1296x729]

images.alt: De'Aaron Fox #4 & Dylan Harper #2 of the San Antonio Spurs high five during the game against the Minnesota Timberwolves during Round Two Game One of the 2026 NBA Playoffs on May 4, 2026 at the Frost Bank Center in San Antonio, Texas.
images.caption: The Spurs took care of business with a big Game 5 win at home against the Timberwolves.
images.name: De'Aaron Fox [1296x729]

images.alt: Jalen Brunson #11 of the New York Knicks celebrates during the game against the Philadelphia 76ers during Round Two Game Four of the 2026 NBA Playoffs on May 10, 2026 at the Wells Fargo Center in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania.
images.caption: The Knicks advanced Sunday with another blowout victory over the 76ers.
images.credit: Jesse D. Garrabrant/NBAE via Getty Images
images.name: Jalen Brunson [1296x729]

images.alt: Anthony Edwards #5 of the Minnesota Timberwolves drives around Stephon Castle #5 of the San Antonio Spurs during the second half of a game in Game One of the Second Round of the NBA Western Conference Playoffs at Frost Bank Center on May 04, 2026 in San A
images.caption: Anthony Edwards returned to the court and helped lead Minnesota to the improbable Game 1 victory.
images.name: Anthony Edwards [1296x729]

images.alt: Detroit Pistons guard Cade Cunningham (2) drives against Cleveland Cavaliers forward Dean Wade, left, during the second half in Game 2 of a second-round NBA playoffs basketball series Thursday, May 7, 2026, in Detroit.
images.caption: Cade Cunningham and the Pistons have raced out to a 2-0 series advantage over the Cavs.
images.name: Cade Cunningham [1296x729]

images.alt: LeBron James #23 of the Los Angeles Lakers works against Luguentz Dort #5 of the Oklahoma City Thunder during the second quarter in Game One of the Second Round of the NBA Western Conference Playoffs at Paycom Center on May 05, 2026 in Oklahoma City, Okla
images.caption: LeBron James and the Lakers are big underdogs against the defending champion Thunder, and Game 1 only lengthened their odds.
images.name: LeBron James  [1296x729]

images.alt: Josh Hart #3 and Jalen Brunson #11 of the New York Knicks high five during the game against the Atlanta Hawks during Round One Game Three of the 2026 NBA Playoffs on April 23, 2026 at State Farm Arena in Atlanta, Georgia.
images.caption: The Knicks have surged to +850 to win the NBA Finals following the first-round exit by the Boston Celtics.
images.name: Jalen Brunson [1296x729]

images.alt: Joel Embiid #21 of the Philadelphia 76ers shoots the ball during the game against the Boston Celtics during Round One Game Seven of the 2026 NBA Playoffs on May 2, 2026 at TD Garden in Boston, Massachusetts.
images.caption: The 76ers' odds to win the NBA Finals have marginally shortened after beating the Celtics in seven games.
images.name: Joel Embiid [1296x729]

images.alt: Jalen Brunson #11 of the New York Knicks drives to the basket during the game against the Atlanta Hawks during Round One Game Six of the 2026 NBA Playoffs on April 30, 2026 at State Farm Arena in Atlanta, Georgia.
images.caption: After entering the playoffs 18-1 to win the NBA championship, the Knicks enter the conference semifinals at 19-1.
images.name: Jalen Brunson [1296x729]

images.alt: Alperen Sengun #28 of the Houston Rockets handles the ball during the game against the Los Angeles Lakers during Round One Game Five on April 29, 2026 at Crypto.Com Arena in Los Angeles, California.
images.caption: Even after pulling out the win over the Lakers on Wednesday night, the Rockets remain big long shots to win the NBA championship.
images.name: Alperen Sengun [1296x729]

images.alt: Victor Wembanyama #1 of the San Antonio Spurs reacts after making a basket against the Portland Trail Blazers during the second quarter in Game Five of the First Round of the NBA Western Conference Playoffs at Frost Bank Center on April 28, 2026 in San An
images.caption: The Spurs saw their title odds move from +450 to +500 after the series-clinching win over Portland.
images.name: Victor Wembanyama [1296x729]

images.name: Cade Cunningham [1296x729]

images.alt: Anthony Edwards
images.caption: The loss of Anthony Edwards put a damper on the Timberwolves' championship odds.
images.name: Anthony Edwards [608x342]

images.caption: The Knicks have dropped from 18-1 to win the NBA championship to 30-1.
images.name: Karl-Anthony Towns [1296x729]

images.alt: Los Angeles Lakers forward LeBron James (23) and forward Rui Hachimura (28) on the court in the first half of game two of the first round of the 2026 NBA Playoffs against the Houston Rockets at Crypto.com Arena.
images.caption: LeBron James and the Los Angeles Lakers were 150-1 long shots to win the title entering their first-round series against the Houston Rockets.
images.name: LeBron James for Odds file (260422) [1296x729]

images.alt: Jalen Brunson #11 of the New York Knicks drives to the basket during the game against the Atlanta Hawks during Round One Game Two of the 2026 NBA Playoffs on April 20, 2026 at Madison Square Garden in New York City, New York.
images.caption: The Knicks fell to 22-1 to win the NBA championship following Monday's Game 2 loss.
images.credit: David L. Nemec/NBAE via Getty Images

images.name: OKC Thunder [1296x729]

images.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander [1296x729]

images.credit: Nathaniel S. Butler/NBAE via Getty Images
images.name: Jayson Tatum [1296x729]

images.caption: The Lakers miss the perimeter shooting that Austin Reaves provides.
images.name: Austin Reaves [1296x729]

images.alt: Luka Doncic #77 of the Los Angeles Lakers handles the ball during the game against the Miami Heat on March 19, 2026 at Kaseya Center in Miami, Florida.
images.caption: The Lakers' championship odds are on the move again after Luka Doncic's injury.
images.name: Luka Doncic [1296x729]

images.alt: Los Angeles Lakers forward LeBron James (23) scores on a breakaway dunk during the first half of an NBA basketball game against the Orlando Magic, Saturday, March 21, 2026, in Orlando, Fla.
images.caption: LeBron James and the Lakers are on a roll as March winds down.
images.name: LeBron James  [1296x729]

images.alt: Victor Wembanyama #1 of the San Antonio Spurs handles the ball during the game against the Detroit Pistons on March 5, 2026 at the Frost Bank Center in San Antonio, Texas.
images.caption: Victor Wembanyama and the Spurs continue to see their championship odds shift.
images.name: Victor Wembanyama [1296x729]

images.alt: Jayson Tatum #0 of the Boston Celtics celebrates after scoring against the Dallas Mavericks during the second quarter at TD Garden on March 06, 2026 in Boston, Massachusetts.
images.caption: The Celtics' title odds have shortened since Jayson Tatum's return last week.
images.name: Jayson Tatum [1296x729]

images.alt: Victor Wembanyama #1 and Devin Vassell #24 of the San Antonio Spurs looks on during the game against the New Orleans Pelicans on January 25, 2026 at the Frost Bank Center in San Antonio, Texas.
images.caption: Led by Victor Wembanyama, the Spurs continue to see their NBA championship odds shorten.
images.name: Victor Wembanyama [1296x729]

images.alt: Cade Cunningham #2 of the Detroit Pistons handles the ball during the game against the Denver Nuggets on February 3, 2026 at Little Caesars Arena in Detroit, Michigan.
images.name: Cade Cunningham [1296x729]

images.alt: Oklahoma City Thunder guard Shai Gilgeous-Alexander (2) against the Phoenix Suns in the first half at Mortgage Matchup Center.
images.caption: Even after a rough stretch of games, the Thunder remain the heavy favorites to win the NBA championship.
images.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander [1296x729]

images.alt: Cleveland Cavaliers guard Donovan Mitchell (45) against the Phoenix Suns in the first half at Mortgage Matchup Center.
images.caption: Adding James Harden to a talented roster with the likes of Donovan Mitchell gives the Cavs a higher upside this season.
images.name: Donovan Mitchell [1296x729]

images.alt: Jaylen Brown #7 of the Boston Celtics shoots against the Portland Trail Blazers at TD Garden on January 26, 2026 in Boston, Massachusetts.
images.caption: The Celtics now have shorter odds to win the NBA title than the Spurs, Rockets, Knicks and Pistons.
images.name: Jaylen Brown [1296x729]

images.alt: Jamal Murray #27 of the Denver Nuggets dribbles the ball during the game against the Dallas Mavericks on January 14, 2026 at American Airlines Center in Dallas, Texas.
images.caption: Jamal Murray has led the Nuggets while Nikola Jokic recovers from injury.
images.name: Jamal Murray  [1296x729]

images.alt: Victor Wembanyama #1 of the San Antonio Spurs looks on during the game against the Cleveland Cavaliers on December 29, 2025 at the Frost Bank Center in San Antonio, Texas.
images.caption: Victor Wembanyama and the Spurs have seen their odds to win the NBA championship shorten in the past week.
images.name: Victor Wembanyama [1296x729]
